lead: Recent developments in climate technology and corporate responses to climate change highlight the ongoing challenges and opportunities in addressing environmental issues.

stories.title: Climate tech companies are going public. What’s next?
stories.summary: A surge of climate tech companies is making headlines as they go public, marking a significant trend in the energy sector. Notably, Solv Energy, a solar and battery company, achieved a $6 billion valuation after its IPO in February. Following this, X-energy, which specializes in small modular nuclear reactors, also went public, indicating a growing investor interest in sustainable energy solutions.

stories.title: The Download: climate tech goes public and the AI Hype Index returns
stories.summary: This edition of The Download discusses the recent trend of climate tech companies going public, alongside the return of the AI Hype Index. The public offerings of companies like Solv Energy and X-energy reflect a broader shift towards investment in technologies aimed at combating climate change. This trend highlights the intersection of climate action and technological innovation.

stories.title: Revealed: the internal BHP memo that slammed the brakes on world’s biggest miner’s climate push
stories.summary: An internal memo from BHP, the world's largest miner, has surfaced, revealing a significant retreat from its previous commitment to climate change mitigation. The document describes climate change as an 'existential' threat but indicates a backtrack on decarbonization efforts across its mining operations. This raises concerns about corporate accountability in the face of climate challenges.

stories.title: The best fans to keep you cool in 2026 – tried and tested
stories.summary: As climate change leads to increasingly frequent heatwaves, finding effective cooling solutions becomes essential. This guide reviews the best fans available in 2026, emphasizing energy efficiency as an alternative to high-energy-consuming air conditioning systems. By opting for fans, consumers can reduce their carbon footprint while staying comfortable during extreme heat.

stories.title: Anthropic’s alliance with pope on AI harms: all in good faith or ‘Vatican-washing?’
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV's recent teaching on artificial intelligence highlights its potential environmental impacts, including exploitation of resources. Anthropic co-founder Chris Olah's presence at the Vatican during this announcement raises questions about the sincerity of corporate engagement with ethical concerns. Critics fear this may lead to superficial discussions rather than substantive action on climate issues.
